questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 10 Ω, Q = 50 Ω and S = 60 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:
questions.solution: At balance P/Q = R/S, so R = P·S/Q = 10×60/50 = 12 Ω.

questions.stem: The balanced Wheatstone bridge shown has P = 100 Ω, Q = 200 Ω and S = 40 Ω (galvanometer reads zero). The unknown resistance R is:

questions.solution: Using line values: multiply by √3 for both star and delta.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The type of a control system is the number of:
options: Poles at the origin of the open-loop transfer function
questions.solution: System 'type' = number of integrators (s-factors) in the denominator at s = 0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Circle diagram:
options: Graphical method to predict motor performance from no-load & blocked-rotor tests
questions.solution: From OC and SC tests. Gives torque, current, power factor, slip at any load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In balanced 3-phase, total instantaneous power:
questions.solution: Balanced 3-phase: sum of three sinusoidal powers = constant (no pulsation).
